ideation: This animation sequence simulates the action of "placing" a pushpin, emphasizing the downward motion and a slight pause at the end.  The robot will use its right arm to perform this action, while the left remains at rest. We'll use the two joints to create a smooth, controlled movement. In the first frame, the right arm is raised slightly, with the hand positioned as if holding the pushpin. The left arm remains at the side. In the second frame, the arm begins to move downward, with a slight forward motion to mimic the act of pushing the pin into a surface. In the third frame, the pin is almost fully inserted, with the arm pausing briefly before returning to a resting position. The final frame shows the arm returning to its resting pose, with the pin presumably in place.

frames.beat: right arm lifts, ready to place the pin
the pin is pushed down with a slight forward motion
arm pauses, the pin is now in place
right arm returns to rest at the side
